**SysCon Synergy 2026** 

SysCon Synergy returned for its **second year**, and once again it turned out to be one of those days that perfectly blended learning, conversations, and a lot of fun with the entire Systems and Control family.

The day started with the **opening address by our HOD**, officially kicking off SysCon Synergy 2026\. The speech set the tone for the day and reminded everyone why events like this matter — they bring together students, researchers, and faculty beyond classrooms and labs.![][image1]

We were also fortunate to have two distinguished guests with us, **Prof. Bijnan Bandyopadhyay** and **Prof. P. S. V. Nataraj**. Both professors shared a few words with us. Their short talks were insightful and inspiring, giving students a glimpse into the broader world of systems and control research and academia.

After the guest speeches, the stage was taken over by the **PhD students**, who shared their thoughts, experiences, and journeys. It was interesting to hear about their research paths, struggles, and little lessons they have picked up along the way.

![][image2]

One of the highlights of the day was the **launch of our resource sharing website — *StateSpace*[(Link)](https://statespace1977.github.io/)**. The website was officially launched by the HOD and is aimed at becoming a place where SysCon students can share resources, blogs, experiences, and learning materials. Along with that, the **SysCon Instagram page** “LooseControl”([Link](https://www.instagram.com/loosecontrol1977/)) was also inaugurated, giving the community a more informal space to stay connected.

![][image3]

By this point, everyone had earned a break — which meant **lunch time**. People gathered in small groups, catching up with friends, chatting with seniors, and even having conversations with the guest professors. It was one of those relaxed moments where academic discussions and casual banter happened at the same table.

![][image4]

Post lunch, things got even more energetic with a **team-building exercise game designed and coordinated by Nagesh sir**. The activity had everyone participating enthusiastically, working in teams, thinking quickly, and occasionally laughing at their own strategies.

![][image5]

As the evening approached, the vibe shifted into celebration mode with **Traditional Day**. Almost everyone showed up in traditional attire, and the campus suddenly looked far more colorful than usual. The evening turned into an open stage where people shared poems, sang songs, and simply enjoyed the moment together.

![][image6]

The day wrapped up the best way possible — **with a delicious dinner and a lot of conversations that didn’t seem to end**.

SysCon Synergy 2026 was not just an event. It was a reminder that beyond equations, controllers, and research papers, the department is really a community of people who learn, support, and celebrate together. And if this was the second edition, we are already excited for what the next one will bring.

![][image7]
